source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
<image1>

Five cards are lying on a table as shown.  Each card has a letter on one side and a whole number on the other side.  Jane said, "If a vowel is on one side of any card, then an even number is on the other side."  Mary showed Jane was wrong by turning over one card.  Which card did Mary turn over?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "as shown," indicating the card faces are provided by an image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false both indicate the visual asset is central
the needed card identities are tied to the shown cards rather than fully described in plain text
multi_step: solving requires applying a conditional rule (vowel -> even) to determine what single card could falsify it
the task involves checking which visible card face is relevant for contradiction, not just direct lookup
multiple candidate cards are presented (5 choices/cards), so the solver must reason across alternatives
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the final response is a single discrete card label/value
the problem is effectively multiple choice with explicit options A-E, making correctness easy to check
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present, supporting reliable downstream validation

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图像均存在，且注册检查通过
初步评分较高，图像依赖性强且具有一定多步推理价值
答案为可验证的短文本，正确性核验成本低
未发现风险标记或明显语义缺口，适合直接进入候选池
